response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience, but the role specifically requires a C++ developer with deep expertise in C++/STL, networking, multi-threading, message brokers, MQTT, and IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity. The resume shows only a brief 7-month stint writing C/C++ at National Instruments back in 2014-2015, with no recent C++ work, no industrial automation background, and no exposure to SDL/IEC 62443 or industrial protocols. Primary expertise is in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/GIS platforms — a fundamental mismatch for an on-premises C++ industrial automation role.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience across diverse stacks
Strong testing/TDD culture and mentoring/workshop background aligns with mentorship duties
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput, async/concurrent systems (DLang at Sociomantic)
concerns: No substantive recent C++/STL experience — only a brief NI role over a decade ago
No exposure to IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No background in industrial automation, MQTT, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850/104, DNP 3.0)

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering seniority and breadth
